summary: Split PR 4/14 for the first stage-specific GR batch touched five stage translation units: Big Blue, Corneria, Mute City, Rainbow Cruise, and Venom. The slice was presented as WIP review prep, locally verified with require-protos, build, and changes_all, and reported matching improvements including 6 new matches and 23 improved unmatched items. Review feedback specifically rejected literal/data ownership work in grcorneria.c where ordinary strings and assert text were promoted into address-named SDATA or packed into a stage data struct.

key_files.role: largest changed file; diff excerpt shows Big Blue matching work, stack padding adjustments, helper use, bitfield/byte access experiments, control-flow reshaping, and matched-score improvements
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: grBigBlue_801E6364, grBigBlue_801E8A1C, grBigBlue_801EB004, grBigBlue_801EB4AC, grBigBlue_801EBAF8, grBigBlue_801EC6C0, grBigBlue_801ECB50
text_corpus issue_comment: improvements for grBigBlue_801EBAF8, grBigBlue_801EB4AC, grBigBlue_801ECB50, grBigBlue_801EB004, grBigBlue_801EC6C0, grBigBlue_801E8A1C, grBigBlue_801E6364

key_files.role: stage TU with one new full match and multiple improvements; received automated preship review comments rejecting literal storage/data ownership edits
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: grCorneria_801DDAC4 reached 100.00%; grCorneria_801DCE1C, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801E03C8, grCorneria_801E25C4 improved

key_files.role: stage TU with two new full matches and several improvements
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: grMuteCity_801F106C and grMuteCity_801F0D20 reached 100.00%; grMuteCity_801F1328, grMuteCity_801F1A34, grMuteCity_801F290C and .sdata2 improved

key_files.role: stage TU with three new full matches and data/code score improvements
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: grRCruise_8020071C, grRCruise_80200154, grRCruise_801FF924 reached 100.00%; .data and other grRCruise items improved

key_files.role: stage TU with matching improvements in Venom functions
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: grVenom_8020362C improved from 77.51% to 92.10%; grVenom_802053B0 improved from 82.77% to 94.77%

what_changed: The PR body states this was a WIP split slice containing only five GR stage files from an aggregate WIP branch.
The bot report recorded overall matched code at 74.57%, up 0.11% and +4080 bytes for the slice.
Six functions reached 100.00% in the bot report: grRCruise_8020071C, grMuteCity_801F106C, grRCruise_80200154, grMuteCity_801F0D20, grRCruise_801FF924, and grCorneria_801DDAC4.
Twenty-three unmatched items improved according to the bot report, concentrated in grbigblue, grmutecity, grvenom, grcorneria, and grrcruise.

Big Blue diff excerpts show iterative matching edits: PAD_STACK size changes, loop initializer changes, replacement of direct HSD_JObj translate field access with HSD_JObjGetTranslation2, local bitfield struct casts for packed fields, declaration-order/lifetime changes, and control-flow/expression reshaping.
Corneria review comments identify added address-named SDATA literal storage and a nested literals union in grCn_Data as rejected preship findings under literals-and-data-ownership.

smart_moves.title: Use existing JObj helper when it expresses the operation
smart_moves.text: In grBigBlue_801E8A1C, the diff replaces an assert plus direct platform->translate copy with HSD_JObjGetTranslation2(platform, &spawn.x8_vec). This aligns with the loaded header-inlines/helper standard when the helper is the source-level operation.

smart_moves.title: Track match-score movement explicitly in todo comments
smart_moves.text: The grBigBlue_801E8A1C todo was updated from 79.9% to 82.96% while preserving notes about remaining addressing-mode and candPtr issues.

smart_moves.title: Run prototype, build, and changes reports for split slices
smart_moves.text: The PR body records python3 configure.py --require-protos, ninja build/GALE01/main.dol, and ninja changes_all, with changes_all reporting only improvements for the slice.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not turn ordinary stage strings or assert text into address-named SDATA for a code-matching slice without explicit data-ownership scope and evidence.
Do not pack literal/assert storage into an unrelated stage data struct to satisfy layout or relocation pressure; leave the literal as source text or fix data ownership separately.

Stack padding, declaration lifetime/order, raw byte casts, and local bitfield overlay structs can move matching scores, but they remain matching tactics that need narrow justification and cleanup consideration rather than becoming broad style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: PAD_STACK changes in grBigBlue_801E6364 and grBigBlue_801EBAF8
raw_diff excerpt: local grBb_* bitfield structs in grBigBlue_801EB004, grBigBlue_801EB4AC, grBigBlue_801EBAF8

decomp_lessons.lesson: For packed state fields in GroundVars, temporary bitfield overlays may explain rlwimi-like codegen, but the slice does not prove they are the preferred final type; curator review should treat them as a tactic or source-update candidate, not an accepted style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: comments 'bitfield rlwimi fixes' and grBb_B4C4HalfBits/grBb_B4C4WordBits casts

naming_conventions.name: Stage prefix symbols
naming_conventions.text: The slice continues existing stage prefixes such as grBigBlue_/grBb_, grCorneria_/grCn_, grMuteCity_, grRCruise_, and grVenom_.

naming_conventions.name: Temporary local role names
naming_conventions.text: Diff excerpts use short local role names such as gp, jobj, child, next, entry, active_idx, random_lane, direction, and ground_gobj where roles are local and clear.

naming_conventions.name: Address-style names retained where semantics are not proven
naming_conventions.text: Bot and diff excerpts retain address-style function/data names such as grBigBlue_801E8A1C, grBb_804D69C8, and grBb_803E2938. The PR evidence does not justify semantic renames for these globals.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: grBigBlue_801E6364 changes PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4); grBigBlue_801EBAF8 changes PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(8) and adds a local u8 pad[8]. This is a matching tactic requiring evidence rather than a style rule.
start_jobj and end_jobj are moved inside the loop in grBigBlue_801EB004, and local variable ordering is changed in grBigBlue_801EBAF8. These are plausible MWCC stack/register-shape tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Bitfield overlays for packed flag/lane updates
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The Big Blue excerpt introduces local bitfield structs for halfword, word, byte, and nibble fields to model lane and flag updates, with comments referencing rlwimi fixes.

The diff reshapes random range assignment in grBigBlue_801E6364, simplifies speed/threshold computations in grBigBlue_801EB4AC, changes assert increment form to count++ <= 1000, and restructures direction/reachability checks.

Some Big Blue excerpts replace structured data access with explicit byte-offset casts into grBb_803E2938 and Ground storage. This may have helped matching but is in tension with typed-field standards unless no real field/type is known.

review_feedback.feedback: The diff adds address-named SDATA storage for ordinary literal strings such as "/GrCn", "jobj.h", "jobj", "gobj", and "gp". Suggested follow-up was to remove new literal globals and keep ordinary strings/assert text inline unless scoped as data ownership work with section/symbol evidence.

The grCn_Data layout was changed to include a nested literals union with char jobj_use_quaternion_assert[0x28]. The review called this hand-modeling literal storage inside an unrelated data struct for layout/relocation rather than authored code. Suggested follow-up was to not pack literal/assert storage into the stage data struct.

follow_up_queries: stage GR literal strings address-named SDATA rejection
GroundVars bigblue x0_w lane bitfield overlay

notes: PR identity, changed files, merge timestamp, local verification commands, and bot match report are directly present in loaded evidence.
Only an excerpt of the large diff is loaded; detailed claims for grcorneria, grmutecity, grrcruise, and grvenom beyond file counts and bot match summaries are limited.
The two review comments are automated preship findings authored through the PR workflow; they are valid PR evidence for QA feedback but should not be overread as independent human reviewer intent.
The PR body reports local verification and no changes_all regressions; this intake preserves that claim but did not independently run builds or objdiff.
Some Big Blue tactics appear to improve matching in the slice, but the evidence does not prove they are accepted final style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Literal promotion is rejected in code-matching stage slices
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2708, grcorneria.c review rejected adding address-named SDATA for ordinary strings such as "/GrCn", "jobj.h", "jobj", "gobj", and "gp". Keep ordinary strings and assert text inline unless the PR is explicitly scoped for data ownership with section/symbol evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not hide literal/assert storage inside unrelated stage data structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2708, grcorneria.c review rejected a nested literals union in grCn_Data containing assert text storage, describing it as hand-modeling literal storage for layout/relocations rather than reconstructing authored code.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR2708 as an exhibit for literal-storage-in-struct rejection
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2708 grcorneria.c as a concrete rejection exhibit under literals-and-data-ownership/no-string-literal-symbol-regression: a nested literals union inside grCn_Data for jobj_use_quaternion_assert was rejected because it packed ordinary literal/assert storage into an unrelated stage data struct to satisfy layout/relocation pressure.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already cover the rule; the PR supplies a useful concrete stage-specific exhibit for curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR2708 as an exhibit for address-named SDATA literal rejection
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2708 grcorneria.c as an exhibit where adding /* literal */ SDATA char grCn_804D4650[8] = "/GrCn" and similar ordinary strings/assert identifiers was rejected for a code-matching slice.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR feedback is a clear example of the existing literal-promotion prohibition.

source_update_candidates.title: Big Blue packed lane/flag fields need typed review
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2708 grbigblue.c used local bitfield overlay structs and u8* Ground casts around offsets 0xC4, 0xC6, and 0xC7 to model lane/flag updates and rlwimi-like codegen. Curator may want a path-scoped note that this area should be reviewed against GroundVars bigblue field ownership rather than treating overlay casts as a preferred final style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence shows repeated packed-field matching tactics in one file, but not enough to promote them as a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all bitfield overlay structs in grbigblue.c were accepted as final style; the loaded evidence only shows they were present in the diff and that match scores improved.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the two loaded automated preship review comments and the final approval record.
Do not promote the PR body's local verification claim into an independent validation result; intake did not rerun configure, build, changes_all, or objdiff.
Do not add semantic names for address-style Big Blue, Corneria, Mute City, Rainbow Cruise, or Venom symbols based only on this slice.
